response.justification: Candidate has 16+ years of software development experience and meaningful exposure to developer tooling (test frameworks like Trial and Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD setup, MCP code generators, internal device orchestration tools), which aligns with the Engineering Productivity focus. However, the resume presents him consistently as an individual contributor (Senior/Staff Software Engineer) with no explicit people management, direct reports, performance reviews, or organizational leadership scope. Mobile-platform ecosystem experience is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015-2016. Worth a screen to probe latent leadership experience, but the gap against the 2-year people management minimum is significant.
strengths: Demonstrated developer-tooling work: test runner/assertion libraries, CI/CD pipelines, MCP frameworks
16+ years of broad software engineering experience with a software-craftsmanship mindset (TDD, pairing, trunk-based dev)
Strong community/teaching record (workshops, dojos, Selenium course) suggesting mentoring ability
concerns: No explicit people management or direct-report leadership experience on the resume
Mobile-platform ecosystem experience is thin and dated (single Apple TV/iOS stint ~2015-2016)
Career trajectory and self-positioning is IC/Staff Engineer, not engineering manager

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test infrastructure background
